assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already been declared and recognised as a receivable/payable, so the exposure is a recognised intragroup monetary item rather than only a forecast transaction.
The receivable/payable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange exposure between group entities with different functional currencies.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge for recognised assets or liabilities, and paragraph 6.3.6 specifically makes foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item a candidate hedged item in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately identifies cash flow hedge for variability in cash flows of a recognised asset or liability, so it is a distinct hedge accounting model that could be assessed for the foreign currency exposure on the recognised receivable.
